rulings.Case Title: 5810-5816-5818 MISSION STREET OWNERS' ASSOCIATION, VS. ANGELA D WARREN et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Plaintiff 5810-5816-5818 Mission Street Owners' Association'S Motion For Terminating Sanctions In The Form Of Dismissal Of Defendant'S Cross Complaint, The Striking Of Defendant'S Answer, And The Rendering Of A Judgment By Default Against Defendant Or, Alternatively, For Issuer And Evidence Sanctions And Request For Monetary Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Hearing set for Monday, June 17, 2013, Line 4: Plaintiff 5810-5816-5818 Mission Street Owners' Association's Motion for Terminating Sanctions in the Form of Dismissal of Defendant's Cross Complaint, the Striking of Defendant's Answer, and the Rendering of a Judgment by Default Against Defendant or, Alternatively, for Issuer and Evidence Sanctions and Request for Monetary Sanctions Pro Tem Judge Anthony Schoenberg, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Motion granted. Terminating sanctions are issued against Defendant as follows: her cross-complaint is dismissed; her answer to Plaintiff's complaint is stricken; and judgment by default is rendered against her. In addition, Defendant shall pay to Plaintiff monetary sanctions in the amount of $1,340.00 within 30 days of this Order. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to tschoenberg@fbm.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: BOBBY JOE CARTER JR. VS. DEPARTMENT OF MOTOR VEHICLES, AN AGENCY OF THE
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Hearing And Amended Petition For Writ Of Mandate, Or In The Alternative, Petition For Writ Of Prohibition From The Suspension Of Petitioner'S California Driver'S License
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, June 17, 2013, Line 5, PETITIONER BOBBY CARTER'S Amended Petition For Writ Of Mandate, Or In The Alternative, Petition For Writ Of Prohibition From The Suspension Of Petitioner'S California Driver'S License Denied. The findings of the hearing officer are supported by the weight of the evidence to warrant suspension per Vehicle Code § 13557(b)(3). The arresting officers had reasonable cause to believe Mr. Carter been driving under the influence of alcohol because he admitted to the police officers that he had been driving and the police officers smelled the strong odor of alcohol on his breath. (Incident Report, p. 6; DS 367, p. 1.) Mr. Carter was lawfully arrested based on his admission and the officer's observations. (Veh. Code § 40300.5.) Mr. Carter had a BAC of more than 0.08; the results of his breath tests were .148 BAC at 2118 hours and .145 BAC at 2120. (Incident Report, p. 6, DS 367, p. 1.) Mr. Carter has not met his burden to rebut the presumption that an official duty has been regularly performed. (Evid. Code § 664.) Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. rulings.Case Title: AMCO INSURANCE COMPANY VS. NELIA INVIERNO AKA NELIA C. INVIERNO INDIVIDUALLY et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn For Order Vacating Default And Default Judgment
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, June 17, 2013, Line 6, DEFENDANT NELIA INVIERNO AKA NELIA C. INVIERNO INDIVIDUALLY Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn For Order Vacating Default And Default Judgment The motion for order vacating default and default judgment is denied without prejudice to Defendant Invierno making this motion again. Defendant Invierno has shown that she lacked actual notice of Plaintiff AMCO's claim. (See Declaration of Invierno 6-9). However, Defendant Invierno has failed to comply with the additional requirement of Civil Procedure Code § 473.5(b) which requires that "The party [Ms. Invierno] shall serve and file with the notice a copy of the answer, motion, or other pleading proposed to be filed in the action." Ms. Invierno did not file a copy of her proposed answer, motion or other pleading along with this Motion to Vacate Default. rulings.Case Title: GABRIEL A. JACKSON et al VS. MARY A. HUGHES, INDIVIDUALLY AND DOING BUSINESS AS et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion For Change Of Venue
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, June 17, 2013, Line 11, DEFENDANT ALLISON AIELLO, INDIVIDUALLY AND DOING BUSINESS AS Notice Of Motion For Change Of Venue The motion for change of venue is denied. Defendant Aiello shows that she resides in Santa Cruz County, but Defendant fails to meet her burden of establishing that San Francisco venue is improper. Defendant fails to show that the injury did not occur in San Francisco. Under Civil Procedure Code §395, San Francisco is a proper venue because the Coe Declaration filed in opposition to the motion indicates that some of the disclosures and invasions of privacy occurred in San Francisco. rulings.Case Title: RED POCKET, INC. VS. 4D GLOBAL PARTNERS LLC
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Arbitration
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, June 17, 2013, Line 21, DEFENDANT 4D GLOBAL PARTNERS, LLC ("4DGP") Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Arbitration The motion to compel arbitration is granted and this action is stayed. Defendant has met its burden of establishing that an agreement to arbitrate exists. First, it is doubtful that the contract is ambiguous. In paragraph 18.6, the parties agreed to arbitrate. In addition, paragraph 18.7 provides that "'prevailing party' means the party determined by the arbitration proceeding to have most nearly prevailed.'" (emphasis added). The purpose of paragraph 18.3 was to provide guidance regarding where any necessary court action, e.g., obtaining a provisional remedy, confirming the arbitration award, would occur. Second, even if the Court finds that the contract is ambiguous, the extrinsic evidence clearly shows that the parties agreed to arbitrate and their only dispute pertained to whether it would take place in San Francisco or Los Angeles. The Court finds that the declarations from Mr. Taylor and Mr. Harker filed in support of the motion to compel arbitration (including exhibits) are more persuasive than Mr. Gordon's declaration filed in opposition to the motion. Finally, it is undisputed that the dispute alleged in the Complaint arises out of the Services Agreement. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =302/MJM
